octodns/record/caa.py.description: Defines CaaValue to parse CAA RDATA text into flags/tag/value, validate field constraints, and provide equality/template support. CaaRecord wires the value type into the octoDNS Record framework and registers it for the CAA RR type.

key_constructs.purpose: Parses 'flags tag value' text (with quoting support) into a structured dict

key_constructs.purpose: Validates flags range and required tag/value presence across all CAA entries

key_constructs.name: CaaValue._equality_tuple
key_constructs.purpose: Provides deterministic equality ordering for comparing record values

key_constructs.name: CaaRecord
key_constructs.purpose: Record implementation for type CAA using CaaValue for per-RDATA handling

key_constructs.name: Record.register_type
key_constructs.purpose: Registers CaaRecord so the system can instantiate it from type name

key_behaviors: parses CAA RDATA text
validates CAA fields
formats CAA RDATA for output
renders templates in CAA value

insights.title: `CaaValue.template` applies parameter formatting with safe short-circuiting
insights.problem: Templating placeholders in record values needed to be handled uniformly and safely.
insights.root_cause: No templating method existed for this value type yet.
insights.solution: Add `template(params)` that returns `self` if `{` is not present; otherwise create a new `CaaValue` and format the `value` string.
insights.lesson_learned: Prefer non-mutating template operations (return new value objects) and include cheap checks (`'{' not in ...`) to reduce formatting overhead and avoid altering plain strings.

insights.title: CAA parsing: allow values containing whitespace/tags by limiting split width
insights.problem: CAA rdata parsing failed for values where the final value portion itself contains whitespace (e.g., additional tag-like fragments).
insights.root cause: The parser split into exactly three fields using `value.split(' ')`, which breaks when the value portion contains spaces.
insights.solution: Changed split to `flags, tag, value = value.split(' ', 2)` to preserve whitespace in the value portion.
insights.lesson_learned: When parsing RFC/text formats where the last field may include spaces or quoted content, cap splits (e.g., `split(' ', 2)`) so earlier delimiters don’t over-split.

insights.title: PlanJson output change: include change record `name`
insights.problem: PlanJson output (and potentially consumers of change JSON) lacked a top-level `name` field per change entry, making it harder to associate changes with specific record names consistently.
insights.root_cause: Change model serialization for create/update/delete did not include record name metadata.
insights.solution: Added `name` to the dict returned by `Create`, `Update`, and `Delete` changes: `self.new.name` for create/update and `self.existing.name` for delete.
insights.lesson_learned: When evolving plan/output schemas, treat serialization changes as potentially breaking for downstream consumers and update tests that validate exact JSON keys.

insights.title: Add Change.data serialization to support checksums
insights.problem: Checksum computation for sync apply needed a stable representation of Create/Update/Delete changes.
insights.root_cause: Change objects lacked a structured data representation usable for hashing and tests.
insights.solution: Implement data properties for Create/Update/Delete returning typed dicts that include existing/new record data as appropriate.
insights.lesson_learned: When using hashing over complex objects, implement explicit, minimal, typed serialization methods rather than hashing repr() or raw objects.

insights.problem: Templating protocol missing for IP value objects.
insights.root_cause: No `template()` on `_IpValue` yet.
insights.solution: Add `template(params)` that checks for `{` and formats with `.format(**params)` when present.
insights.lesson_learned: String-like value wrappers should implement `template()` similarly to other value types for consistent formatting behavior.

insights.title: `SshfpValue.template` formats fingerprint values (with a likely-inverted placeholder check)
insights.problem: Fingerprint templating implementation appears to have an incorrect placeholder check, risking early return when placeholders exist (depending on exact code).
insights.root_cause: The added logic uses `if '{' in self.fingerprint: return self`, which is opposite of the pattern used in other value classes (they return early when `{` is not present).
insights.solution: Implemented `template()` for `SshfpValue` but the condition likely should be `if '{' not in self.fingerprint: return self`.
insights.lesson_learned: When implementing repetitive logic across many value types, copy the pattern carefully—an inverted conditional can silently disable templating for one record type.

insights.title: `TlsaValue.template` formats certificate association data
insights.problem: TLSA templating placeholders weren’t supported for `certificate_association_data`.
insights.root_cause: Missing `template()` on TLSA value type.
insights.solution: Add `template(params)` that checks for `{` and formats `certificate_association_data` on a new instance.

insights.problem: No templating support for DS digest values containing `{}` placeholders.
insights.root_cause: Missing per-type template implementation.
insights.solution: Implement `template(params)` to short-circuit when `{` is absent; otherwise format `digest` and return a new instance.
insights.lesson_learned: Implement templating in each value class but keep behavior consistent (short-circuit + return new instance) to avoid surprising mutations.

insights.title: Add --checksum CLI option for plan integrity gating
insights.problem: Operators applying sync changes may need a mechanism to ensure the apply phase matches the previously computed plan exactly.
insights.root_cause: The CLI previously lacked any integrity constraint tying apply to a computed plan.
insights.solution: Introduce --checksum option and pass checksum through to Manager.sync. Update help text to clarify --doit (dry-run/force) interactions when Manager.enable_checksum is used.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding new safety flags, propagate them end-to-end (CLI -> Manager.sync) and update help/behavior messaging so users understand precedence and required inputs.

insights.title: Extend CAA tests to cover whitespace-containing value fields
insights.problem: Previous tests assumed parsing would fail when extra whitespace words were present.
insights.root cause: Tests reflected the old parsing behavior that incorrectly rejected valid rdata with spaces in the value field.
insights.solution: Updated/added assertions so that `CaaValue.parse_rdata_text('1 tag value another')` parses successfully and includes `'value another'` as part of the value, and also validated quoted cases with additional words.
insights.lesson_learned: If you change parsing rules to be more permissive (to correctly handle spec/real-world input), adjust tests to assert the new acceptance behavior and preserve correct segmentation of fields.

tests/config-semis/escaped.semis.yaml.short_description: Fixture testing escaped semicolons in YAML values
tests/config-semis/escaped.semis.yaml.description: Provides test DNS record values where semicolons are escaped (\;) inside TXT content. It ensures parsing/serialization preserves literal semicolons when escaping is used.

insights.title: Fixture covers already-escaped semicolons for comparison
insights.problem: Semicolon escaping behavior can regress by double-escaping or by skipping transformations incorrectly depending on the `escaped_semicolons` flag.
insights.root_cause: Different input formats require different expected outcomes.
insights.solution: Fixture includes TXT values with already escaped `\;` sequences.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep paired fixtures for “already escaped” vs “not escaped” inputs to validate flag behavior thoroughly.

README.md.short_description: Project overview and documentation links for octoDNS
README.md.description: Describes octoDNS as a DNS-as-code tool for managing records across multiple providers using a pluggable architecture. Links to full documentation and contribution guidance, but does not define runtime behavior.

insights.title: Documented new infomaniak provider
insights.solution: Added provider listing entry in README.
insights.lesson_learned: N/A

requirements.txt.short_description: Pinned Python dependencies for octoDNS development/testing
requirements.txt.description: Lists version-pinned runtime and development dependencies used to build, test, and run the project. It includes constraints for supported Python versions and excludes unsupported versions via environment markers.

tests/config/include/dict_too.yaml.short_description: Fixture used to test !include behavior with out-of-order keys
tests/config/include/dict_too.yaml.description: A small YAML fixture intentionally containing keys in an order that should not affect include-loading behavior. It helps verify that !include files are parsed with the same loader/class as their parent so configuration inheritance works correctly.

tests/config/include/main.yaml.short_description: Root YAML fixture composing multiple !include inputs
tests/config/include/main.yaml.description: Builds a main configuration by including array, dict, empty, nested, and subdir YAML fragments via !include tags. Exists to validate include mechanics and loader consistency across nested included files.

tests/config-semis/unescaped.semis.yaml.short_description: Fixture testing unescaped semicolons in YAML values
tests/config-semis/unescaped.semis.yaml.description: Provides test DNS record values where semicolons appear unescaped in TXT content. It is used to verify that parsing either fails or behaves differently than the escaped fixture, depending on expected escaping rules.

insights.title: Fixture covers unescaped semicolons including a lone ';' entry
insights.problem: Edge cases around semicolon handling may fail if logic only escapes when semicolons appear in certain contexts.
insights.root_cause: String escaping rules can have subtle behavior for edge cases (e.g., `;` only).
insights.solution: Fixture includes `value` with an unescaped semicolon and a list containing both an unescaped semicolon string and a standalone `;`.
insights.lesson_learned: Include extreme boundary strings in provider fixtures to detect partial or incorrect escaping logic.

tests/config/dump-processors.yaml.short_description: Test config defining processors and a dump target provider
tests/config/dump-processors.yaml.description: Configures YAML providers for input and output (dump) along with processors such as TypeAllowlistFilter and a custom CountingProcessor. Defines two zones: one with a valid processor chain and another that references an unknown processor to exercise error handling.

tests/config/dynamic-config.yaml.short_description: Test config for dynamic manager/config fields and None handling
tests/config/dynamic-config.yaml.description: Sets up multiple YAML providers and multiple zones, aiming to ensure that dynamic configuration fields (including optional None values) do not crash the manager. Includes empty sections for manager secret handlers and plan outputs to exercise robustness in config parsing.

insights.title: Add config fixture to verify YAML null values don't break initialization
insights.problem: Initialization bugs only reproduce when config uses explicit `null` values for optional keys.
insights.root cause: Without a fixture containing explicit `null`s, regressions could slip through tests that only cover missing keys.
insights.solution: Added a test fixture including keys with empty values (interpreted as None) such as `manager:`, `secret_handlers:`, `processors:`, `post_processors:`, `plan_outputs:` and ensured provider zone processors config also includes a `processors:` key with no value.
insights.lesson_learned: For configuration normalization code, include fixtures that represent the problematic but realistic case: explicit YAML `null` rather than omitted keys.

tests/config-secrets.yaml.short_description: Test configuration for secret handlers with env and cross-handler dependency rules
tests/config-secrets.yaml.description: Defines secret_handlers using helper.DummySecrets with three variants: a plain dummy prefix, an env-backed prefix, and a case meant to fail because it depends on other handlers configured with nondeterministic order. Provides required placeholder keys (providers/zones) for the manager to load.

tests/config/alias-zone-loop.yaml.short_description: Test config exercising alias zone loops in zone resolution
tests/config/alias-zone-loop.yaml.description: Creates zones that alias other zones, including an alias chain that loops (alias-loop.tests. -> alias.tests. -> unit.tests. is fine, while alias-loop can be made cyclic via configuration). Used to verify the system detects and handles alias cycles rather than recursing indefinitely.

tests/config/always-dry-run.yaml.short_description: Test config for per-zone always-dry-run behavior
tests/config/always-dry-run.yaml.description: Configures input/output YAML providers and sets always-dry-run on one zone while explicitly disabling it on a subzone. Used to validate correct precedence and propagation of dry-run behavior in the planning/apply workflow.

tests/config/bad-plan-output-config.yaml.short_description: Fixture: invalid plan_outputs configuration for PlanLogger class
tests/config/bad-plan-output-config.yaml.description: Defines manager.plan_outputs with a PlanLogger subclass but includes an invalid field to trigger configuration validation errors. Used to ensure the CLI/manager rejects bad plan output configuration early.

tests/config/plan-output-filehandle.yaml.short_description: Fixture: plan_outputs entry referencing a nonexistent plan logger class
tests/config/plan-output-filehandle.yaml.description: Defines manager.plan_outputs with a key mapping to octodns.provider.plan.DoesntExist, which should trigger import/instantiation failure. Exists to verify configuration validation for plan output backends.

tests/config/processors-missing-class.ya.short_description: Fixture: processors list contains entry with missing class name
tests/config/processors-missing-class.ya.description: Includes providers plus a processors section where one provider setup is valid but zones reference a 'noop' and another processor scenario uses missing class definitions. Exists to test processor resolution errors when configured classes are not found.

tests/config/processors-wants-config.yam.short_description: Fixture: processor configured with class that requires params (missing config)
tests/config/processors-wants-config.yam.description: Configures a processor wants-config using helpers.WantsConfigProcessor without required parameters, expecting the system to detect missing configuration. Used to verify processor config validation and helpful error messaging.

tests/config/processors.yaml.short_description: Fixture: processor registration, selection, and counting coverage
tests/config/processors.yaml.description: Defines multiple processor classes (noop, test, global-counter) and attaches them to zones, including a zone that references an invalid processor class. Exists to test end-to-end processor loading, application order, and reporting/coverage for provider/processor version output.

tests/config/provider-problems.yaml.description: Defines providers and several zones that reference missing sources or targets, unknown source/target identifiers, and a non-targetable provider source relationship. Used to ensure the system validates zone wiring between sources and targets and surfaces precise configuration errors.

tests/config/simple-alias-zone.yaml.short_description: Fixture: zone aliasing from one.tests to unit.tests
tests/config/simple-alias-zone.yaml.description: Configures a sources/targets workflow for unit.tests and defines alias.tests. as an alias of unit.tests. Exists to verify that zone alias expansion produces identical behavior as the referenced zone.

tests/config/simple-arpa.yaml.short_description: Fixture: auto-arpa generation for IPv4/IPv6 reverse zones
tests/config/simple-arpa.yaml.description: Enables manager.auto_arpa to populate PTR zones for both IPv4 and IPv6 reverse lookups and sends results to the dump provider. Used to verify reverse-zone naming, generation correctness, and TTL/replace behavior across ARPA types.

tests/config/simple-split.yaml.short_description: Fixture: SplitYamlProvider with multiple targets and subzones
tests/config/simple-split.yaml.description: Uses SplitYamlProvider for sources and multiple dump targets (dump and dump2) and configures subzones and an empty zone. Exists to test splitting behavior across provider instances and ensure correctness when the same target file might be written multiple times.

tests/config/simple-validate.yaml.short_description: Minimal YAML config used to validate provider/zone wiring.
tests/config/simple-validate.yaml.description: Defines two providers (a YAML source and a simple helper provider) and maps zone unit.tests. with both as sources and dump as the target. Used by tests to ensure configuration loads and validates correctly with a small surface area.

tests/config/simple.yaml.short_description: Integration-style YAML config exercising multiple providers and targets.
tests/config/simple.yaml.description: Configures manager threading plus several providers (input YAML, dump targets, and helper providers like geo/nosshfp/simple). The zone unit.tests. uses multiple targets including dump2 to exercise multi-target behavior and subzone handling.

tests/config/unit.tests.yaml.short_description: Fixture YAML containing a full set of DNS records for unit.tests.
tests/config/unit.tests.yaml.description: Provides record definitions for unit.tests. including varied RR types (A/AAAA/NS/SRV/MX/TXT/LOC/CNAME/DNAME/PTR/SPF/NAPTR/URLFWD) and nested subdomain records. Includes octodns-specific per-record metadata for excluded/ignored/included behaviors to test record filtering and planning logic.

tests/config/unknown-processor.yaml.short_description: Config fixture to test behavior when an unknown processor is referenced.
tests/config/unknown-processor.yaml.description: Defines in/dump providers but includes a non-existent processor name under zones.unit.tests. to force a validation/error path. Used to ensure octoDNS rejects or errors on unknown processors during config parsing/validation.

tests/config/unknown-provider.yaml.short_description: Config fixture to test behavior when a zone references a missing source provider.
tests/config/unknown-provider.yaml.description: Defines a YAML provider and a simple_source provider, but the zone unit.tests. references a non-existent source name under sources. This checks error handling for unknown providers/sources when building zone populations.

tests/config/unknown-source-zone.yaml.short_description: Config fixture to test error when an alias points to an unknown zone.
tests/config/unknown-source-zone.yaml.description: Configures providers for unit.tests. normally, then adds alias.tests. with an alias to does-not-exists.tests. to force alias resolution failure. Exists to validate zone alias handling and required referenced zone existence.

docs/api.rst.description: Creates the API documentation toctree for developer interfaces and related lifecycle documentation. It exists to organize and link to the detailed API reference pages under docs/api/*.

docs/configuration.rst.short_description: Main configuration guide covering providers, zones, and options.
docs/configuration.rst.description: Explains YAML configuration structure, including YamlProvider options, dynamic vs static zone config, and manager/provider knobs like include_meta, max_workers, lenient, and strict_supports. Serves as a central reference for how to structure octoDNS config files.

docs/dynamic_records.rst.short_description: Documentation for dynamic/geo/weighted DNS record configuration.
docs/dynamic_records.rst.description: Describes how to express dynamic records (geo routing, weighted pools, healthcheck/fallback) and provides an annotated example configuration. Also covers geo code formats, subnet targeting precedence, healthcheck options, and operational behavior across providers.

docs/dynamic_zone_config.rst.short_description: Guide to matching and generating managed zones dynamically.
docs/dynamic_zone_config.rst.description: Explains dynamic zone configuration by using wildcards in the zones section and matching modes (legacy, file-glob, regex) to claim zones returned by sources. Exists to help users reduce config duplication and safely control which zones are managed.

docs/getting-started.rst.short_description: Tutorial walkthrough for setting up and running octoDNS.
docs/getting-started.rst.description: Provides step-by-step instructions to install octoDNS, create provider/zone config, define a simple record YAML, and run octoDNS sync in noop then apply modes. Includes workflow guidance (PR/noop/deploy) and examples of syncing between providers and using dynamic sources.

docs/records.rst.short_description: Documentation describing record models, validation, and record-level behaviors.
docs/records.rst.description: Covers record configuration semantics for YamlProvider, supported/unsupported record behavior across providers, lenience modes, and advanced record type extensibility. Also documents AutoArpa PTR generation, restrictions, and record/zone level lenient handling.

docs/zone_lifecycle.rst.short_description: Describes zone planning/apply lifecycle and key hooks.
docs/zone_lifecycle.rst.description: Documents how Manager.sync orchestrates Zone creation, source population, processor transformations, provider desired/existing normalization, plan generation, safety checks, and apply. Serves as a reference for contributors to understand the lifecycle and where provider/processor overrides occur.

tests/config/dynamic-arpa/unit.tests.yam.short_description: Fixture zone YAML for dynamic arpa/PTR-related tests.
tests/config/dynamic-arpa/unit.tests.yam.description: Contains a small set of A/AAAA records plus an extra record not meant to be treated as a zone file name, used to test PTR/AutoArpa or dynamic arpa generation behavior. Exists to isolate ARPA-related logic in a tight fixture.

tests/config/override/dynamic.tests.yaml.short_description: Fixture YAML providing overrides for dynamic.tests records.
tests/config/override/dynamic.tests.yaml.description: Defines record overrides for a dynamic.tests zone, including replacing record 'a' with new A values and adding a new 'added' A record. Used to verify override/merge behavior in dynamic record test scenarios.

tests/zones/2.0.192.in-addr.arpa..short_description: Fixture DNS reverse zone with SOA, NS, and PTR records
tests/zones/2.0.192.in-addr.arpa..description: Defines a small RFC1918 reverse DNS zone (2.0.192.in-addr.arpa) for tests. It includes SOA metadata, two authoritative NS records, and multiple PTR mappings to verify parsing/processing of PTR data.

tests/zones/ext.unit.tests.extension.short_description: Fixture DNS extension zone with SOA and NS records only
tests/zones/ext.unit.tests.extension.description: Provides an additional test DNS zone under ext.unit.tests. with SOA and NS records. It exists to validate that the system can load and handle zone files that contain minimal record sets (no data beyond delegation metadata).

tests/zones/invalid.records.tst.short_description: Fixture DNS zone containing intentionally questionable/invalid records
tests/zones/invalid.records.tst.description: Defines an invalid/edge-case DNS zone with many record types (SRV, TXT, MX, A/AAAA, CNAME) with inconsistent or malformed content. It is used to exercise the validator and error-handling paths by feeding problematic record definitions.

tests/zones/invalid.zone.tst.short_description: Fixture DNS zone with only SOA to trigger invalid-zone behavior
tests/zones/invalid.zone.tst.description: Creates a minimal zone file (invalid.zone.) containing an SOA but no further records. This exists to test how the system reacts to structurally incomplete zones during validation or planning.

tests/zones/unit.tests.tst.short_description: Primary DNS zone fixture with broad record-type coverage
tests/zones/unit.tests.tst.description: Defines the main unit.tests. DNS zone used by tests, including SOA/NS plus many record types (SSHFP, CAA, SRV, TXT, MX, LOC, A/AAAA, CNAME, SVCB, HTTPS). It serves as a comprehensive input to verify end-to-end parsing, normalization, and plan generation across diverse DNS data models.

docs/api/cmds.rst.short_description: API docs index for CLI commands
docs/api/cmds.rst.description: Sphinx documentation entrypoint that uses autosummary to list octoDNS CLI command modules. It helps publish generated API reference for commands like dump/report/sync/validate/versions.

docs/api/helpers.rst.short_description: API docs index for octoDNS helper modules
docs/api/helpers.rst.description: Sphinx autosummary page that enumerates helper modules (context, deprecation, equality, idna, yaml). It exists to generate and organize the API reference for cross-cutting utilities used throughout the project.

docs/api/processors.rst.short_description: API docs for processor configuration and available processors
docs/api/processors.rst.description: Explains how to configure processors in the octoDNS config file, including per-zone and global application via the manager section. It then links to autosummary pages for each processor class (meta/filter/ownership/etc).

docs/api/providers.rst.short_description: API docs index for provider base/plan/yaml modules
docs/api/providers.rst.description: Sphinx autosummary page that references the provider interfaces/implementations (base, plan, yaml). It supports API documentation for how octoDNS models desired vs. planned DNS changes across different provider types.

docs/api/records.rst.short_description: API docs index for supported DNS record types
docs/api/records.rst.description: Sphinx autosummary list of octoDNS record classes covering many RR types (A/AAAA/CNAME/SSHFP/SVCB/HTTPS/TXT/etc). It exists to provide discoverable API documentation for the internal record model used by sources, processors, and plans.

docs/api/secrets.rst.short_description: API docs index for secret handling modules
docs/api/secrets.rst.description: Sphinx autosummary page listing secret-related classes (base, environ, exception). It documents how octoDNS represents secrets and integrates them into configuration-driven provider operations.

docs/api/sources.rst.short_description: API docs index for DNS source modules
docs/api/sources.rst.description: Sphinx autosummary index for source modules such as envvar and tinydns, plus the source base interface. It exists to document how octoDNS ingests DNS state/config from different upstream representations.

tests/config/split/dynamic.tests.tst/a.y.short_description: Dynamic A record split fixture with geo pools and weights
tests/config/split/dynamic.tests.tst/a.y.description: YAML config snippet defining dynamic A records for name 'a' with geo-based rules mapping to pools. Pools contain weighted IP values and the rules choose the pool based on geos (EU, EU-GB, NA region groups, or default pool iad).

tests/config/split/dynamic.tests.tst/aaa.short_description: Dynamic AAAA record split fixture with geo pools and weights
tests/config/split/dynamic.tests.tst/aaa.description: Defines dynamic AAAA records for 'aaaa' with weighted IPv6 pool values and geo-based rule selection. It exists to test correct translation of dynamic weighted pools for AAAA records, including multiple rules and a default pool.

tests/config/split/dynamic.tests.tst/cna.short_description: Dynamic CNAME split fixture with geo pools and weighted targets
tests/config/split/dynamic.tests.tst/cna.description: YAML config snippet for dynamic CNAME records under 'cname', using geo rules to select among pools of weighted alias targets. It also specifies a static/explicit value for the base record name, enabling tests of mixed dynamic and fixed behaviors in the config processing pipeline.

tests/config/split/dynamic.tests.tst/rea.short_description: Fixture for dynamic A records with geo rules and weighted regional pools
tests/config/split/dynamic.tests.tst/rea.description: Defines a realistic dynamic DNS config for an A record with per-region weighted pools. Includes geo-based routing rules mapping multiple geo codes to specific pools.

tests/config/split/dynamic.tests.tst/sim.short_description: Fixture for dynamic CNAME weighted routing with a single default pool
tests/config/split/dynamic.tests.tst/sim.description: Creates a minimal dynamic DNS configuration for a CNAME record using one weighted pool and a single rule. Serves as a small baseline test for weighted selection behavior.

tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/$unit..short_description: Fixture defining multiple DNS record types for the unit.tests zone
tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/$unit..description: Contains a YAML mapping for a variable/split zone file that defines A/CAA/NS/SSHFP records. Used to validate how the system interprets multiple RR types and their structured fields (including geo-specific values for A).

tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/_srv._.short_description: Fixture for SRV record values under _srv._tcp subdomain
tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/_srv._.description: Defines an SRV RRset with TTL and multiple targets including port, priority, and weight. Used to test parsing and rendering of SRV-specific fields.

tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/mx.yam.short_description: Fixture for MX record set with preferences and multiple exchanges
tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/mx.yam.description: Defines TTL and an MX RRset with several exchange targets and increasing preferences. Used to validate MX RRset handling and ordering/serialization of preference values.

tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/naptr..short_description: Fixture for NAPTR record values with regex and service fields
tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/naptr..description: Defines a NAPTR RRset with TTL and multiple entries specifying flags, order, preference, regexp, and service. Exists to ensure complex string/regex-based fields are parsed and preserved correctly.

tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/txt.ya.short_description: Fixture for TXT record set including escaped/complex strings
tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/txt.ya.description: Defines a TXT RRset with TTL and multiple text values, including a DKIM-like value with escaped semicolons. Used to validate correct YAML escaping and DNS TXT serialization.

tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/urlfwd.short_description: Fixture for URLFWD records with redirect codes and masking
tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/urlfwd.description: Defines TTL and multiple URLFWD values containing HTTP-like redirect codes, masking, path, query, and target URL. Used to verify URLFWD-specific schema and field parsing.

tests/config/include/dict.yaml.short_description: Included dictionary fixture used by YAML include tests
tests/config/include/dict.yaml.description: A simple key/value mapping (k, m, z) used as an include target in other config fixtures. Provides a baseline dict structure for merge and include behaviors.

tests/config/include/include-array-with-.short_description: Fixture testing !include of a mix of array and dict files
tests/config/include/include-array-with-.description: Uses the custom !include tag to include array.yaml and dict.yaml into a single data list. Exists to confirm the include mechanism supports heterogeneous included content.

tests/config/include/include-array-with-.short_description: Fixture that includes a missing file to test include error handling
tests/config/include/include-array-with-.description: Attempts to include array.yaml and a non-existent does-not-exist.yaml file using !include. Used to validate that the system surfaces a clear error when includes cannot be resolved.

tests/config/include/include-array-with-.short_description: Fixture that includes an unsupported nested path target
tests/config/include/include-array-with-.description: Includes subdir/value.yaml via !include to represent an unsupported include target type/path. Used to ensure the include implementation rejects unsupported include patterns.

tests/config/include/include-dict-with-a.short_description: Fixture testing !include used to build a dict that includes multiple files
tests/config/include/include-dict-with-a.description: Uses !include with an array of inputs (dict.yaml and array.yaml) under a single dict key (data). Exists to test whether the include tag can be used in dict contexts with mixed content.

tests/config/include/merge.yaml.short_description: Fixture validating YAML merge keys with an included dict
tests/config/include/merge.yaml.description: Defines a parent dict that uses YAML merge (<<) with !include dict.yaml, then overrides/extends values like child and z. Used to verify merge semantics with included content.

insights.title: New fixture validates YAML merge precedence with !include
insights.problem: Need a concrete YAML fixture to verify that `<<: !include ...` merges included dict content and that keys are overridden in the expected order.
insights.root_cause: Transition from legacy include syntax to merge operator-based include required a new canonical example.
insights.solution: Commit 45e372a adds merge.yaml where parent keys include both included dict and locally defined overrides, asserting precedence: included keys, then overridden by parent, and locally defined keys added.
insights.lesson_learned: Add small, focused fixtures that encode precedence rules when changing merge/include semantics.

tests/config/bad-plan-output-missing-cla.short_description: Bad-plan-output fixture missing required plan output class
tests/config/bad-plan-output-missing-cla.description: Provides a malformed manager.plan_outputs configuration where a plan output entry ('bad') is an empty mapping. Used to ensure validation fails when required fields like the plan output class are missing.

tests/config/bad-provider-class-module.y.short_description: Config fixture for provider class with missing module path
tests/config/bad-provider-class-module.y.description: Defines a providers section referencing a non-existent provider class in a module that cannot be imported. Exists to verify config validation/error handling for bad class import paths.

tests/config/bad-provider-class-no-modul.short_description: Config fixture for provider class with missing module qualifier
tests/config/bad-provider-class-no-modul.description: Defines a provider class name that lacks the expected module path. Exists to ensure the loader rejects improperly qualified provider class references.

tests/config/bad-provider-class.yaml.short_description: Config fixture for provider class that does not exist
tests/config/bad-provider-class.yaml.description: References a fully-qualified provider class that does not exist. Exists to confirm the system surfaces errors when resolving provider classes by name fails.

tests/config/empty.yaml.description: A minimal/empty YAML file used as a fixture. Exists to test behavior when no configuration is provided.

tests/config/missing-provider-class.yaml.short_description: Config fixture with provider missing required 'class' field
tests/config/missing-provider-class.yaml.description: Defines a provider entry without a class attribute under the providers key. Exists to verify schema validation catches missing required provider configuration.

tests/config/missing-provider-config.yam.short_description: Config fixture with provider class but no other required provider settings
tests/config/missing-provider-config.yam.description: Specifies a provider class but omits required configuration fields for that provider type. Exists to ensure provider instantiation fails or warns when mandatory settings are absent.

tests/config/missing-sources.yaml.short_description: Config fixture with zones present but missing sources configuration
tests/config/missing-sources.yaml.description: Provides an empty providers section while still declaring a zone entry keyed as if sources were required. Exists to validate that the system rejects configurations lacking required source/provider definitions.

tests/config/sub.txt.unit.tests.yaml.short_description: Unit test fixture YAML placeholder for sub.txt tests
tests/config/sub.txt.unit.tests.yaml.description: A YAML fixture file that is effectively empty and likely used to test include/substitution mechanics. Exists to provide a baseline or missing-content case for unit tests.

tests/config/subzone.unit.tests.yaml.short_description: DNS record fixture for a subzone in unit tests
tests/config/subzone.unit.tests.yaml.description: Defines a set of A records within a subzone using numeric and string keys (e.g., '2', '12', 'test'). Exists to supply deterministic DNS data for unit tests around zone loading and record rendering.

tests/config/unordered.yaml.short_description: YAML fixture with intentionally unordered record fields
tests/config/unordered.yaml.description: Provides two record definitions with keys ordered differently (e.g., value before type for one record). Exists to ensure the parser/order-independence of record field handling.

tests/config/hybrid/one.test.yaml.short_description: Hybrid zone file fixture containing a TXT record
tests/config/hybrid/one.test.yaml.description: Declares a TXT record named 'flat-zone-file' with a specific string value. Exists as a focused fixture for tests covering hybrid/flat-zone behavior.

tests/config/include/array.yaml.short_description: YAML fixture providing an array include target
tests/config/include/array.yaml.description: Contains a top-level YAML sequence of integers. Exists to test include handling for array values.

tests/config/include/empty.yaml.short_description: YAML fixture providing an empty include target
tests/config/include/empty.yaml.description: An empty YAML file intended to be included via a custom include mechanism. Exists to validate how the loader handles includes that resolve to no content.

tests/config/include/include-doesnt-exis.short_description: YAML fixture referencing a non-existent include file
tests/config/include/include-doesnt-exis.description: Uses a custom YAML tag (!include) to include a file that does not exist. Exists to verify that missing include targets produce clear errors.

tests/config/include/nested.yaml.short_description: YAML fixture for nested include usage via custom tag
tests/config/include/nested.yaml.description: Uses !include to include another file located in a subdirectory, enabling nested include scenarios. Exists to test include path resolution and recursive/nested include behavior.

tests/config/split/shared.yaml.short_description: Fixture record for shared TXT value used in split configuration tests
tests/config/split/shared.yaml.description: Provides a DNS TXT record definition labeled only-shared for split-mode tests. It is intended to be included only when shared-file processing is enabled, exercising conditional config inclusion.

tests/config/split/unit.tests.yaml.short_description: Fixture record for unit TXT value used when zone-file processing is enabled
tests/config/split/unit.tests.yaml.description: Defines a TXT record named only-zone-file for unit.tests fixtures in split-mode scenarios. It exists to validate that zone-file processing includes this record when enabled.

docs/api/manager.rst.short_description: Sphinx API doc entry for octodns.manager module
docs/api/manager.rst.description: Includes an automodule directive to generate API documentation for the octodns.manager module. It connects the module’s docstrings to the published documentation site.

docs/api/zone.rst.short_description: Sphinx API doc entry for octodns.zone module
docs/api/zone.rst.description: Uses Sphinx automodule to generate API documentation for the octodns.zone module. This file ensures Zone-related classes are surfaced in the docs via introspection.

tests/config/hybrid/two.test./$two.test..short_description: Hybrid fixture for a root-hierarchy TXT record under two.test.
tests/config/hybrid/two.test./$two.test..description: Defines an inline YAML fixture for the two.test. zone where the root label ('') has a TXT record value of root TXT. It supports hybrid configuration tests that need special handling for root-level records.

tests/config/hybrid/two.test./split-zone.short_description: Hybrid fixture for split-zone-file TXT record under two.test.
tests/config/hybrid/two.test./split-zone.description: Provides a TXT record named split-zone-file with a fixed value for hybrid tests. It is used to verify behavior when zones are loaded/merged from a split zone-file source.

tests/config/include/subdir/value.yaml.short_description: Included YAML fragment containing plain text fixture content
tests/config/include/subdir/value.yaml.description: A small YAML file used as an included fixture fragment within test configurations. It exists to validate include/subdir loading behavior where YAML content is incorporated into larger test configs.

tests/config/split/subzone.unit.tests.ts.short_description: Subzone fixture defining A record for label '12' in unit.tests.tst
tests/config/split/subzone.unit.tests.ts.description: Defines an A record for the '12' label within subzone.unit.tests.tst with value 12.4.4.4. It supports split configuration tests that verify per-subzone file loading and record rendering.

tests/config/split/subzone.unit.tests.ts.short_description: Subzone fixture defining A record for label '2' in unit.tests.tst
tests/config/split/subzone.unit.tests.ts.description: Provides an A record for the '2' label in subzone.unit.tests.tst with value 2.4.4.4. This fixture exercises split behavior for numeric subzone labels.

tests/config/split/subzone.unit.tests.ts.short_description: Subzone fixture defining A record for label 'test' in unit.tests.tst
tests/config/split/subzone.unit.tests.ts.description: Defines an A record for the test label in subzone.unit.tests.tst with value 4.4.4.4. It exists to validate consistent loading of non-numeric subzone record files in split mode.

tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/aaaa.y.short_description: Fixture defining an AAAA record for unit.tests.tst
tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/aaaa.y.description: Declares an AAAA record named aaaa with ttl 600 and the specified IPv6 value. This fixture ensures IPv6 record types are parsed and included correctly in split-mode tests.

tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/cname..short_description: Fixture defining a CNAME record for unit.tests.tst
tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/cname..description: Defines a CNAME record named cname with ttl 300 pointing to unit.tests. This exists to test correct parsing and formatting of domain-targeting records in split configurations.

tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/dname..short_description: Fixture defining a DNAME record for unit.tests.tst
tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/dname..description: Declares a DNAME record named dname with ttl 300 pointing to unit.tests. This fixture validates support for DNAME record types in the test split pipeline.

tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/exclud.short_description: Fixture for a CNAME record marked as excluded via octodns metadata
tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/exclud.description: Defines a CNAME record excluded with a nested octodns.excluded list containing 'test'. It exists to verify that octodns exclusion metadata filters out records during processing.

tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/ignore.short_description: Fixture for an A record marked ignored via octodns metadata
tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/ignore.description: Defines an A record ignored with octodns.ignored set to true. This fixture ensures the processor skips records that are explicitly marked ignored in metadata.

tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/includ.short_description: YAML fixture defining an included CNAME record for unit.tests.tst
tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/includ.description: Provides a split-config test fixture with an included domain section and a CNAME target/value. It exists to validate how the system merges or interprets nested 'included' blocks during config splitting.

tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/ptr.ya.short_description: YAML fixture defining a PTR record for unit.tests.tst
tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/ptr.ya.description: Defines a PTR record with TTL and a list of values for reverse-DNS style mapping. It exists to test that PTR records are parsed and split correctly, including list handling.

tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/spf.ya.short_description: YAML fixture defining an SPF record for unit.tests.tst
tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/spf.ya.description: Specifies an SPF TXT-like record with TTL and an SPF value string. It exists to ensure SPF records are treated as expected record types and values during config splitting.

tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/sub.ya.short_description: YAML fixture defining an NS record with multiple targets
tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/sub.ya.description: Defines an NS record type for a subdomain with multiple name server targets and no TTL specified. It exists to validate multi-value record parsing for NS records and behavior when TTL is omitted.

tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/www.su.short_description: YAML fixture defining an A record for www.sub
tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/www.su.description: Provides an A record with TTL for the www.sub subdomain pointing to a single IPv4 value. It exists to test that split configs create the correct FQDN record entries and preserve TTL/value fields.

tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/www.ya.short_description: YAML fixture defining an A record for www
tests/config/split/unit.tests.tst/www.ya.description: Defines a standalone www A record with TTL and an IPv4 value. It exists as a counterpart to subdomain fixtures to verify consistent parsing across different relative names.

tests/config/split/unordered.tst/abc.yam.short_description: YAML fixture defining a basic A record for abc
tests/config/split/unordered.tst/abc.yam.description: Declares an A record with a single value using a straightforward key order. It exists to confirm that record parsing is order-independent for basic fields like type/value.

tests/config/split/unordered.tst/xyz.yam.short_description: YAML fixture defining an A record with unordered keys
tests/config/split/unordered.tst/xyz.yam.description: Defines an A record where 'value' appears before 'type', with a comment noting that t comes before v. It exists to ensure YAML parsing and record construction do not depend on key ordering.

tests/config/dynamic-arpa/3.2.2.in-addr..short_description: Empty YAML fixture for dynamic ARPA zone 3.2.2.in-addr.arpa
tests/config/dynamic-arpa/3.2.2.in-addr..description: An intentionally empty configuration file representing a dynamic reverse-lookup (in-addr.arpa) zone. It exists to test how the system handles absent/empty zone definitions during dynamic ARPA processing.

tests/config/dynamic-arpa/b.e.f.f.f.d.1..short_description: Empty YAML fixture for dynamic IPv6 ARPA zone
tests/config/dynamic-arpa/b.e.f.f.f.d.1..description: An intentionally blank YAML file keyed by a long ip6.arpa domain name, representing a dynamic IPv6 reverse zone. It exists to verify that the dynamic ARPA loader tolerates empty zone configs and still recognizes the zone file.

octodns/record/svcb.py.short_description: Implements RFC 9460 SVCB record parsing/validation/rendering and supported SvcParams.
octodns/record/svcb.py.description:
This module defines the Svcb record value model used by octodns for DNS records of type `SVCB`. It encapsulates both the top-level SVCB/HTTPS RDATA fields (priority, targetname, and a set of key/value SvcParams) and the parsing/validation rules needed to convert between octodns' internal representation and textual RDATA.

The core abstraction is `SvcbValue`, a dict-like value object mixed with `EqualityTupleMixin`. It provides:
- `parse_rdata_text` to parse an rdata-text string into structured fields.
- `validate` to check semantic correctness (priority range, target FQDN, and SvcParam validity depending on whether the record is AliasMode priority=0).
- `rdata_text` to render internal values back to canonical-ish rdata-text ordering (sorted by key_num).

Supported SvcParams are declared in the `SUPPORTED_PARAMS` mapping. Each entry can define `key_num` (used for ordering), `has_arg` (whether the param must/not carry an argument), and optionally `validate` and `parse_rdata_text` handlers for that param type. For list-valued SvcParams, helper functions enforce that the internal value is a Python list (e.g., `alpn`, `ipv4hint`, `ipv6hint`, `mandatory`) and the rdata parser uses a comma-splitting hook. The module also includes ECH support (`ech`) which is validated as base64.

At the bottom, `SvcbRecord` ties the value model into the octodns record framework by setting `_type = 'SVCB'` and `_value_type = SvcbValue`, then registering it via `Record.register_type`. This is how other parts of the system (Zone ingestion, provider diffs, plan/apply) learn how to construct and compare SVCB records.

key_constructs.name: SUPPORTED_PARAMS
key_constructs.purpose: Authoritative definition of supported SvcParam keys, their ordering (key_num), arg presence, and handlers.
key_constructs.reasoning: Editors must treat this mapping as the schema/behavior source for parsing and validation. Adding/changing SvcParams must align parsing, validation, ordering, and list/scalar typing expectations.

key_constructs.name: validate_svcparam_alpn
key_constructs.purpose: Validates that `alpn` is a list and each element conforms to SVCB ALPN chunked value rules.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures list typing is enforced before per-element validation by `_ChunkedValue.validate`, so wrong-type inputs produce deterministic validation reasons.

key_constructs.name: validate_svcparam_ipv4hint
key_constructs.purpose: Validates that `ipv4hint` is a list of valid IPv4 address strings.
key_constructs.reasoning: The error strings include the key name `ipv4hint` and the invalid address value; any edits must preserve message semantics to keep test expectations stable.

key_constructs.name: validate_svcparam_ipv6hint
key_constructs.purpose: Validates that `ipv6hint` is a list of valid IPv6 address strings.
key_constructs.reasoning: Matches the same list typing and IP parsing approach as v4, with IPv6-specific error messaging.

key_constructs.name: validate_svcparam_mandatory
key_constructs.purpose: Validates `mandatory` is a list of supported SvcParam identifiers (or `keyNNNN`).
key_constructs.reasoning: It enforces that `mandatory` members must be either supported keys or begin with `key`, and also validates `key` numeric ranges when applicable.

key_constructs.name: validate_svcparam_ech
key_constructs.purpose: Validates that `ech` SvcParam value is base64-decodable (strict).
key_constructs.reasoning: ECH validation is type-agnostic (expects a scalar string) and relies on `b64decode(..., validate=True)` for strictness.

key_constructs.name: SvcbValue
key_constructs.purpose: Represents the structured SVCB RDATA: svcpriority, targetname, and svcparams; provides parsing, validation, and rendering.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the single point where octodns' internal data model is translated to/from text and where semantic constraints (AliasMode vs ServiceMode, list typing, key ordering) are applied.

key_constructs.name: SvcbRecord
key_constructs.purpose: Registers the SVCB DNS record type with octodns record framework using SvcbValue as its value type.
key_constructs.reasoning: Editing this requires awareness of the framework expectations around `_type`, `_value_type`, and record registration.

key_behaviors: parses SVCB rdata-text into svcpriority, targetname, and svcparams dict
validates svcpriority range and targetname FQDN semantics
enforces AliasMode rule: svcpriority=0 forbids non-empty svcparams
validates SvcParam argument presence/absence based on has_arg
validates list-typed SvcParams (alpn, ipv4hint, ipv6hint, mandatory) as Python lists
renders SvcbValue back to rdata_text with SvcParam keys sorted by key_num

pitfalls.mistake: Treating list-valued SvcParams as scalars during parsing or setting them directly in tests/values.
pitfalls.consequence: Validation will emit `'<key> is not a list'` reasons, and rdata rendering may not match the expected comma-joined form.
pitfalls.prevention: When providing/altering values for `alpn`, `ipv4hint`, `ipv6hint`, or `mandatory`, ensure they are Python lists so `rdata_text` and `_equality_tuple` behave as intended.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing validation error message strings or key names (e.g., ipv4hint vs ip4hint) without updating corresponding tests.
pitfalls.consequence: Unit tests that assert exact `ValidationError.reasons` will fail due to mismatch in expected reason text.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the exact wording and formatting of reasons produced by validate_* helpers.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering AliasMode constraints (svcpriority==0) or forgetting that validation forbids svcparams on AliasMode records.
pitfalls.consequence: Records that previously passed will start failing validation, or vice versa, affecting record creation and plan generation.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the invariant enforced in `SvcbValue.validate`: for svcpriority==0, any non-empty `svcparams` is invalid.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying SvcParam key ordering logic used by `rdata_text` and `_equality_tuple`.
pitfalls.consequence: Round-trip parse->render tests and equality/hash behavior may differ because ordering and representation affect `rdata_text` and equality tuples.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect `svcparamkeysort` (key_num and `keyNNN` handling) and the way list vs scalar is rendered.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the contract of `parse_rdata_text` for quote handling or comma splitting.
pitfalls.consequence: Quoted list values or quoted scalar values may parse incorrectly; tests assert specific handling of quoted params and comma-joined rdata list formatting.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the quote stripping behavior in `parse_rdata_text_svcparamvalue_list` and the special handling in `SvcbValue.parse_rdata_text`.

key_sections: SUPPORTED_PARAMS: defines schema/handlers for each SvcParam and drives ordering + validation
SvcbValue.parse_rdata_text: splits rdata tokens into svcpriority, targetname, and svcparams; applies per-param parse hooks
SvcbValue.validate: applies global SVCB rules (priority, targetname) and per-SvcParam validation/arg rules
SvcbValue.rdata_text / _equality_tuple: defines rendering order and equality semantics
skip_unless_needed: SvcbValue.template: only templating targetname; svcparams are explicitly not templated
validate_svckey_number: only affects validation of `keyNNNN`-style mandatory keys

relevant_snippets.description: Covers SvcbRecord construction for AliasMode (svcpriority=0) and ServiceMode (svcpriority>0), plus changes() diff behavior and __repr__.
Covers SvcbValue.parse_rdata_text error cases, double key rejection, list parsing, quoted target/quoted list parameter handling, and rdata_text round-trip.
Covers EqualityTupleMixin-based comparisons and hash behavior for SvcbValue.
Covers Record.new validation expectations: missing/invalid priority, missing/invalid targetname, AliasMode svcparams restriction, unknown param handling, and specific SvcParam validation messages.

related_files.relationship: Unit tests assert exact parsing, rendering, comparison, and validation reason strings for SvcB/SvcParam logic.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any modification to parsing/validation semantics or error messages in this module can require synchronized updates to expected test outputs.

data_constants_to_check: octodns/record/svcb.py: SUPPORTED_PARAMS (key schema, key_num ordering, has_arg defaults)

insights.title: Delegate SVCB targetname validation to validate_target_fqdn() and accept templated values safely
insights.problem: SVCB target validation logic was duplicated and inconsistent with other record types, and templated values could be treated incorrectly.
insights.root_cause: Inline FQDN/trailing-dot/targetname checks existed in SvcbValue instead of using the shared target validator.
insights.solution: Updated SvcbValue to call `validate_target_fqdn(value.get('targetname'), _type, 'targetname')` and rely on shared behavior for templating bypass and trailing dots.
insights.lesson_learned: Centralized validation reduces drift, especially around edge cases like templating and null targets.

insights.title: `SvcbValue.template` formats targetname and leaves svcparams as future work
insights.problem: Templating partially needed for SVCB record fields; full semantics for svcparams weren’t implemented.
insights.root_cause: SvcbValue is composite; only `targetname` templating behavior was clearly defined.
insights.solution: Add `template(params)` to format `targetname` when it contains `{`, and add a TODO comment about whether/how to template `svcparams`.
insights.lesson_learned: When full support is unclear, implement a minimal safe subset with explicit TODOs rather than guessing and breaking record equivalence.

insights.title: SVCB/HTTPS: normalize certain SvcParam values to lists and support list parsing in rdata_text
insights.problem: SVCB SvcParam fields that are inherently list-valued (e.g., ALPN, ipv4hint/ipv6hint, mandatory) were not consistently represented as lists, and parsing from rdata text didn't produce list types when required.
insights.root cause: Validation expected `svcparamvalue` to be a list, but parsing logic still stored the first element as a scalar; additionally, earlier functions split strings directly and didn't normalize types.
insights.solution: Introduced list validation helper (`validate_list`) and ensured `validate_svcparam_alpn`, `validate_svcparam_iphint`, and `validate_svcparam_mandatory` use it. Renamed ip4hint/ip6hint validators to ipv4hint/ipv6hint. Added parsing hook `parse_rdata_text_svcparamvalue_list` and wired it into `SUPPORTED_PARAMS` for list-valued keys so `parse_rdata_text` produces list values from comma-separated rdata input. Updated rendering (`rdata_text` and set conversion) to join list values with commas.
insights.lesson_learned: For record formats with structured parameters, ensure consistent internal types end-to-end: parse -> validate -> render should agree on whether a field is scalar or list to avoid silent schema drift.

octodns/provider/yaml.py.short_description: Provider that loads and writes DNS zone records from YAML files on disk.
octodns/provider/yaml.py.description:
This module defines `YamlProvider`, a `BaseProvider` implementation responsible for sourcing zone records from YAML files and optionally acting as a target to write planned record changes back to disk. It supports multiple record behaviors (geo, dynamic, pool status, dynamic subnets, multivalue PTR) by advertising them via the class-level SUPPORTS_* flags and by dynamically reporting supported record types from `Record.registered_types()`.

The provider’s core lifecycle is `populate()`, which discovers YAML inputs for a given `Zone` (optionally split across multiple files via `split_extension`, plus an optional shared YAML file and the zone’s own file unless disabled). It reads YAML via `safe_load`, normalizes each loaded entry into a list (if necessary), injects a default TTL when missing, performs conditional semicolon escaping/unescaping for TXT/SPF when `escaped_semicolons` is toggled, and then creates `Record` instances (`Record.new`) to add into the zone (`zone.add_record`). For output, `_apply()` reconstructs a deterministic representation of the full zone after applying the plan to a copy of the existing zone (`plan.existing.copy()` + `copy.apply(plan.changes)`), then writes either a single zone YAML file or a split file set. The split output groups records by record-name file (with special catch-all handling) under a per-zone subdirectory.

The file also contains `SplitYamlProvider`, which is a deprecated wrapper around the newer split mechanism; callers should still respect its behaviors because existing configurations/tests may rely on it. Key subtle semantics include: `populate()`’s behavior when no sources exist (it raises unless `ignore_missing_zones=True`, but it does not treat `target=True` as “ignore state”), deterministic ordering of sources, and filename handling for IDNA vs UTF-8 zone names via separate UTF-8 and IDNA paths. When modifying this code, keep in mind that tests validate not only in-memory record correctness but also written YAML structure (including singular vs plural `value(s)` fields) and the correctness of TXT/SPF semicolon escaping with the `escaped_semicolons` flag.

key_constructs.name: YamlProvider
key_constructs.purpose: Source/target provider that loads and writes DNS zone YAML record definitions.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the central abstraction that both reads zone YAML into `Zone.records` and renders plan-applied changes back to YAML files, including split-zone support and TXT/SPF semicolon escaping semantics.

key_constructs.name: list_zones
key_constructs.purpose: Enumerate zone names discoverable from the provider’s configured directory.
key_constructs.reasoning: Its discovery rules must stay consistent with how `populate()` locates sources, and it is directly exercised by tests for both split/non-split and ignore/disable combinations.

key_constructs.name: populate
key_constructs.purpose: Load YAML record definitions into the supplied `Zone`, optionally returning existence status.
key_constructs.reasoning: Controls the primary read path: source discovery (split extension, zone file, shared file), error/ignore behavior when nothing is found, and per-record normalization before `Record.new`.

key_constructs.name: _apply
key_constructs.purpose: Apply a plan to a copy of existing records and write resulting YAML to disk.
key_constructs.reasoning: Writes full-zone snapshots (not just diff records), enforces deterministic ordering, and implements split-zone output including catch-all file behavior and TTL/semicolon serialization rules.

key_constructs.name: _split_sources
key_constructs.purpose: Yield YAML file paths for split-style zones under a per-zone subdirectory.
key_constructs.reasoning: Implements UTF-8 vs IDNA subdirectory detection and raises if both versions exist; correctness is important for split-zone populate behavior.

key_constructs.name: _zone_sources
key_constructs.purpose: Return the single YAML filename for a non-split zone, preferring/handling UTF-8 vs IDNA.
key_constructs.reasoning: Defines which zone file is used when both UTF-8 and IDNA filenames could exist, matching expectations in IDNA tests.

key_constructs.name: SplitYamlProvider
key_constructs.purpose: Deprecated split-style provider alias (superseded by `YamlProvider(split_extension=...)`).
key_constructs.reasoning: Even though deprecated, it likely still exists for backward compatibility; altering it can break existing configurations/tests that import it.

key_behaviors: discovers zone YAML inputs via directory scan and split_extension rules
optionally ignores missing zone inputs when ignore_missing_zones=True
loads YAML using safe_load with enforce_order/order_mode
creates records via Record.new and merges them into the zone with replace semantics via populate_should_replace
supports TXT/SPF semicolon escaping/unescaping controlled by escaped_semicolons
writes either single or split YAML zone output files based on split_extension
applies plan changes to plan.existing.copy() to render a full snapshot for output
writes split output with catch-all file rules for specific record names

pitfalls.mistake: Changing semicolon escaping behavior without handling both directions (populate and _apply) for TXT/SPF
pitfalls.consequence: Round-trips will not preserve record values correctly; tests asserting YAML file content for escaped/unescaped semicolons can fail.
pitfalls.prevention: When adjusting semicolon logic, ensure the transformation in populate() and serialization in _apply() remain inverses under escaped_semicolons flag settings.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying filename/IDNA selection logic (UTF-8 vs IDNA paths) without preserving the 'both exist' validation
pitfalls.consequence: populate() may silently pick the wrong file or tests expecting ProviderException will fail.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the explicit checks in _split_sources and _zone_sources that raise when both UTF-8 and IDNA directories/files exist.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing output semantics from 'full zone snapshot' to 'diff-only' writes
pitfalls.consequence: Applied YAML may omit records that weren’t part of the change list, breaking round-trip assumptions and future plans.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the copy/apply/serialize sequence in _apply() that materializes the whole zone from `plan.existing`.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering source discovery rules such that list_zones() and populate() diverge
pitfalls.consequence: Discovery tests or real usage may list zones that cannot be populated (or vice versa).
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the directory scanning trimming and extension filtering logic used by list_zones() in relation to split_extension behavior.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing split output naming (record file naming and catch-all filename conventions)
pitfalls.consequence: Targets may write to unexpected filenames; split-zone tests and interoperability could break.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect `CATCHALL_RECORD_NAMES` and the `$[zone].yaml` catch-all behavior when split_catchall is enabled.

key_sections: YamlProvider.__init__: config parameters that affect discovery and serialization
list_zones: directory scanning logic for split and non-split zone names
_split_sources / _zone_sources: UTF-8 vs IDNA path selection and 'both exist' errors
_populate_from_file: YAML parsing, TTL defaulting, Record.new creation, semicolon transformations
_apply: plan application to copy + deterministic serialization + split/non-split file writing
skip_unless_needed: The large module docstring examples (documentation only)
Deprecated SplitYamlProvider details unless handling backward compatibility

relevant_snippets.description: Covers provider populate as source vs target, round-trip expectations, and existence/plan behavior.
Validates IDNA handling: prefers correct file, writes UTF-8 filenames, and raises when both exist.
Covers ignore_missing_zones: raises when disabled; returns False and loads nothing when enabled, including multi-source mix.
Covers enforce_order / un-sorted YAML raising ConstructorError when enforce_order=True.
Validates list_zones behavior for split_extension variations and disable_zonefile.
Exercises _split_sources including UTF-8 vs IDNA directory conflict and deterministic ordering of yielded YAML files.

related_files.relationship: Integration/behavior tests for populate/apply, idna handling, split discovery, and missing-zone behavior.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any behavioral change in populate/apply/source discovery/escaping/split output naming is validated here.

insights.title: Add ignore_missing_zones parameter to YamlProvider
insights.problem: YamlProvider raised an exception when used as a source and no zone YAMLs were found for a zone; this is undesirable in multi-source setups where some directories may not contain the zone.
insights.root_cause: The provider hard-failed in populate() when sources were empty and no target was present, without a way to opt out for “missing is ok” scenarios.
insights.solution: Introduced an ignore_missing_zones boolean (default False) stored on YamlProvider and used to guard the populate() exception: it now raises only when not ignore_missing_zones.
insights.lesson_learned: When implementing “merge multiple sources” behavior, provide configuration knobs to prevent hard failures for expected absence conditions (and cover multi-directory scenarios in tests).

insights.title: YamlProvider option escaped_semicolons=false unescapes \\; in TXT/SPF values
insights.problem: When `escaped_semicolons` was disabled, values still contained escaped semicolons (`\;`), resulting in incorrect TXT/SPF record content output.
insights.root_cause: YamlProvider previously escaped semicolons for specific record types; logic did not unescape when the user requested the opposite behavior.
insights.solution: In `YamlProvider` processing, when `not self.escaped_semicolons` and record type is `SPF` or `TXT`, it replaces `\;` with `;` for both `value` and each element of `values` before writing output data.
insights.lesson_learned: For escaping/unescaping toggles in configuration-driven exporters, ensure both directions are handled and cover list-valued and scalar-valued fields in tests.

insights.title: Fix unescaped semicolons handling in TXT/SPF values
insights.problem: When `escaped_semicolons=False`, semicolons in TXT/SPF values were not consistently escaped, and an earlier bug also referenced the wrong key (`d['']` instead of `d['value']`).
insights.root_cause: The provider preprocessing for TXT/SPF values had a keying mistake (`d[''].replace(...)`) and therefore would fail to transform `d['value']` properly for unescaped inputs.
insights.solution: In the loop that processes each record dict `d`, replace semicolons in `d['value']` and in each string of `d['values']` with escaped `\;` when `escaped_semicolons` is disabled. Also corrected the conditional formatting for readability.
insights.lesson_learned: When mutating provider config dictionaries, be careful to use the correct field keys—typos like `d['']` can silently break parsing. Add targeted tests for boundary cases (e.g., `;` at end, lone `;` value).

insights.title: YamlProvider.populate now returns exists based on loaded sources; target no longer suppresses reading
insights.problem: Previously, when acting as a target, YamlProvider.populate ignored existing records, forcing a plan that assumed non-existence.
insights.root_cause: populate had special-case logic: if target was set, it returned False early and ignored the zone’s loaded state.
insights.solution: Remove the early return for target; compute exists = len(sources) > 0 and return exists after populating; improve logging to include exists.
insights.lesson_learned: Be careful with populate semantics when distinguishing Source vs Target roles—tests should ensure that the provider correctly reports existence state without unintentionally discarding loaded zone records.

insights.title: YamlProvider.apply reworked to apply changes to a copy of existing zone
insights.problem: Apply logic previously assumed limited visibility into existing state (and only created records from changes), which breaks for operations requiring full zone reconstruction.
insights.root_cause: YamlProvider._apply built output records from change objects rather than applying changes to a complete zone state.
insights.solution: Create a copy = plan.existing.copy(), call copy.apply(plan.changes), then write sorted(copy.records) to YAML output files, using copy.decoded_name for filenames.
insights.lesson_learned: For renderers that must output a whole zone snapshot, apply changes to a materialized zone state (copy) rather than assembling partial records from the diff.

insights.title: YamlProvider no longer copies record._octodns into output structure
insights.problem: YamlProvider redundantly/manually copied `_octodns` into YAML output, risking duplication/overwriting with new record.data semantics.
insights.root_cause: Provider previously assumed it needed to inject `_octodns` into serialized output instead of relying on shared record serialization behavior.
insights.solution: Removed logic that added `d['octodns'] = record._octodns` when serializing records.
insights.lesson_learned: Avoid duplicating serialization of shared metadata in providers; rely on canonical `Record.data` / serialization pathways so future fixes (like `Record.data` including octodns) automatically propagate.

octodns/processor/templating.py.short_description: Processor that applies python .format-style templating to record values.
octodns/processor/templating.py.description:
This module defines a `Templating` processor used in the octodns processing pipeline to substitute placeholders in record values using a `template(params)` interface. It exists to let users write template strings in zone/record definitions (typically TXT and similar single-value records, but also multi-value records such as MX/SRV via their value objects) and have those placeholders expanded using built-in variables like `{zone_name}` and `{record_fqdn}` plus any extra user-provided `context`.

The main entrypoint is `process_source_and_target_zones(desired, existing, provider, lenient=False)`. It builds a `zone_params` dict from the desired zone (derived from `desired.decoded_name` / `desired.name`) and then, for each record in `desired.records`, creates a record-specific parameter dict via `build_params(record)`. For each value, it calls `value.template(params)` and catches `KeyError` to raise a `TemplatingError` that includes record FQDN context and the missing placeholder. If templating results differ from the original value(s), the processor replaces the record on the `desired` zone using `record.copy(..., lenient=lenient)` and `desired.add_record(..., replace=True, lenient=lenient)`, while honoring an effective `lenient` flag combined from the processor’s configured `self.lenient` (inherited) and the method argument.

A key behavior is the `trailing_dots` configuration. When `trailing_dots` is disabled, the processor strips the final `.` from all built-in FQDN-related parameters it provides (`zone_name`/decoded/encoded variants and `record_*fqdn` variants). This impacts what placeholder expansions produce and is therefore part of the effective templating API. Additionally, the processor detects whether record value objects support templating by checking for a `template` attribute; if the value type doesn’t support templating, templating is skipped for that record/value rather than erroring. The `context` passed to the processor supports static literals and dynamic callables; callable context values are evaluated as `v(desired, provider)` at processing time and merged into `zone_params` so they can be used in `.format` placeholders.

key_constructs.name: TemplatingError
key_constructs.purpose: Domain-specific exception for undefined template parameters during rendering.
key_constructs.reasoning: The processor wraps raw `KeyError` from `value.template(params)` with record identity and the missing placeholder name to produce stable error messages consumed by tests and likely user-facing behavior.

key_constructs.name: Templating
key_constructs.purpose: Applies placeholder templating to desired records during planning/sync preparation.
key_constructs.reasoning: It owns the parameter-building logic (zone/record params, trailing dot handling, dynamic context evaluation) and performs conditional record replacement only when rendered values differ.

key_constructs.name: process_source_and_target_zones
key_constructs.purpose: Iterates desired zone records and renders templates for supported value objects.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the pipeline hook where templating is applied; it mutates the `desired` zone by replacing records when templating changes their values, while preserving leniency semantics and erroring deterministically via `TemplatingError` when placeholders are missing.

key_behaviors: builds built-in template parameters from desired zone and per-record attributes
evaluates user-provided `context` callables as `v(desired, provider)`
renders templates via `value.template(params)` when the value supports templating
skips templating for value types lacking a `template` method
replaces records in the desired zone when rendered values differ
raises `TemplatingError` on undefined template keys with record fqdn context
optionally strips trailing dots from built-in FQDN template variables

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming all value objects have a `.template` method.
pitfalls.consequence: The processor will skip templating for those records/values, leading to unexpanded placeholders and surprising final plans.
pitfalls.prevention: When changing value types or processor behavior, ensure templating support is explicit via a `template` attribute (as expected by current logic).

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the semantics of `trailing_dots` variables inconsistently across zone and record parameters.
pitfalls.consequence: Template outputs will drift from expected forms; tests assert exact parameter dictionaries (not just final strings).
pitfalls.prevention: Keep trailing-dot handling consistent for all `zone_*` and `record_*fqdn` parameters used in templates.

pitfalls.mistake: Letting raw `KeyError` propagate instead of wrapping in `TemplatingError`.
pitfalls.consequence: Error messages become unstable and tests expecting `TemplatingError` strings will fail; users lose record-specific context.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the `KeyError` -> `TemplatingError(record, ...)` conversion behavior.

pitfalls.mistake: Replacing the processor’s update flow without preserving leniency propagation.
pitfalls.consequence: Record addition/copying may raise validation errors when templates produce values that require `lenient` mode (or conversely validation may be skipped too aggressively).
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure any record replacement continues to pass `lenient` through both `record.copy(..., lenient=...)` and `desired.add_record(..., lenient=...)`.

key_sections: TemplatingError: how missing placeholders are reported
Templating.__init__: configuration knobs (`trailing_dots`, `context`)
process_source_and_target_zones: zone param building, record param building, value templating/rendering, and conditional record replacement
skip_unless_needed: Large docstring examples (they describe usage but do not affect runtime logic)

relevant_snippets.description: Defines dummy `CustomValue` and custom Record types (`Single`, `Multiple`) to test templating attribute detection and non-templatable behavior.
test_cname: verifies `{zone_name}` substitution occurs and that unrelated records remain unchanged.
test_txt: verifies single-value templating and correct placeholder expansion using `{zone_num_records}` and `{zone_name}`.
test_no_template: asserts templating is skipped when value type lacks `.template` and checks that `.template` is queried on supported custom values.
test_trailing_dots: patches `TxtValue.template` and asserts the exact `params` dict passed when `trailing_dots=False`.
test_context: verifies static context, dynamic callable context evaluation (`lambda _, __`), and provider-dependent callables.
test_bad_key: asserts missing placeholder errors raise `TemplatingError` with the expected message for both TXT and CNAME (lenient record).

related_files.relationship: Unit tests validate templating outputs, trailing dot behavior, context evaluation, and error messages from this processor.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to parameter construction or error wrapping should be validated against exact expected strings and dict structures asserted in tests.

insights.title: Ensure templated record modifications propagate lenient mode to forced copies
