insights.solution: Added `__version__` while keeping `__VERSION__` as an alias (`__version__ = __VERSION__ = '1.2.1'`), and documented deprecation in changelog.
insights.lesson_learned: For public constants, provide aliasing during transition and update tests/tooling together to avoid mismatched expectations.

octodns/yaml.py.short_description: YAML safe load/dump with !include support and optional key-order enforcement.
octodns/yaml.py.description:
This module centralizes YAML parsing/serialization behavior for the octodns configuration system. It defines specialized PyYAML loader/dumper subclasses that enforce deterministic mapping key ordering (natural or simple lexicographic) and attach additional context information to constructed mappings for better diagnostics. This is primarily used when loading YAML configuration files where stable output and strict ordering checks help prevent subtle configuration drift.

A key feature is the custom handling of the `!include` YAML tag. `ContextLoader` implements `construct_include` (loading a single included file) and `flatten_include` so that `!include` can participate in YAML merge semantics (`<<:`). The implementation uses PyYAML flattener hooks: it overrides/implements flattening methods (ported from an upstream PyYAML PR approach) and registers flatteners/constructors for `tag:yaml.org,2002:seq`, `tag:yaml.org,2002:map`, and `!include`. Included content is resolved relative to the directory of the YAML file being parsed via the current node’s mark (`node.start_mark.name`).

It also defines `SortEnforcingLoader`, with concrete subclasses `NaturalSortEnforcingLoader` and `SimpleSortEnforcingLoader`, which validate that mapping keys appear in the YAML document in the expected order. When order enforcement is enabled, `safe_load()` selects a loader based on `order_mode`; otherwise it uses `ContextLoader`. For output, `SortingDumper` sorts mapping keys before dumping (again supporting `natural` or `simple` modes) via custom representers, and `safe_dump()` selects the appropriate dumper and uses fixed dump options (indentation and explicit document start).

key_constructs.name: ContextLoader
key_constructs.purpose: PyYAML SafeLoader subclass adding ContextDict context and custom !include + merge flattening.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the base loader for octodns YAML semantics; it handles both `!include` resolution and wraps mappings with line/column context used in error reporting.

key_constructs.name: SortEnforcingLoader
key_constructs.purpose: SafeLoader subclass that validates YAML mapping keys are in sorted order during construction.
key_constructs.reasoning: It enforces a deterministic key order at parse time. The loader raises `ConstructorError` with 'keys out of order' diagnostics when the document order differs from the expected order.

key_constructs.name: NaturalSortEnforcingLoader
key_constructs.purpose: SortEnforcingLoader variant that uses natsort key generation for expected ordering.
key_constructs.reasoning: It defines `KEYGEN` using natsort so that strings with embedded numbers sort naturally (e.g., '*.2.2' before '*.10.1').

key_constructs.name: SimpleSortEnforcingLoader
key_constructs.purpose: SortEnforcingLoader variant that uses raw string comparison for expected ordering.
key_constructs.reasoning: It treats ordering as straightforward lexical ordering, enabling a distinct, test-covered parse-time contract.

key_constructs.name: InvalidOrder
key_constructs.purpose: Exception raised when safe_load/safe_dump receive an unknown order_mode.
key_constructs.reasoning: It provides a stable, user-facing error message listing valid modes; tests assert exact strings.

callers.context: from octodns.yaml import InvalidOrder, safe_dump, safe_load

key_constructs.name: safe_load
key_constructs.purpose: Load YAML from a stream with optional key-order enforcement and ContextDict-wrapped mappings.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the module’s primary public entry point for parsing; it chooses loader class based on enforce_order/order_mode and delegates to `yaml.load` with that loader.

callers.context: from octodns.yaml import InvalidOrder, safe_dump, safe_load

key_constructs.name: SortingDumper
key_constructs.purpose: SafeDumper subclass that sorts mapping keys before dumping.
key_constructs.reasoning: It ensures YAML output is deterministic and matches the parse-time sorting expectations used elsewhere in the system.

key_constructs.name: NaturalSortingDumper
key_constructs.purpose: SortingDumper variant using natsort key generation.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures numeric-aware natural ordering for dumped YAML mappings; tests compare exact dump output strings.

key_constructs.name: SimpleSortingDumper
key_constructs.purpose: SortingDumper variant using raw string comparison.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides a predictable alternative to natural sorting; tests validate both parsing and dumping behavior.

key_constructs.name: safe_dump
key_constructs.purpose: Dump Python data to a YAML stream using deterministic ordering and fixed formatting options.
key_constructs.reasoning: It selects an order_mode-specific dumper and uses consistent dump options (notably `explicit_start=True`) that are asserted in tests.

callers.context: from octodns.yaml import InvalidOrder, safe_dump, safe_load

key_behaviors: constructs ContextDict-wrapped mappings during YAML load with line/column context
resolves !include paths relative to the YAML file currently being parsed
integrates !include with YAML merge syntax (<<) via custom flattening behavior
optionally enforces mapping key ordering during parse and raises ConstructorError when out of order
sorts mapping keys before YAML dump using natural or simple ordering
raises InvalidOrder when order_mode is not one of the supported loader/dumper modes

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the behavior of !include without preserving how flattening integrates with YAML merge operator (<<).
pitfalls.consequence: Composite configurations that rely on YAML merge semantics can silently break or produce incorrect merged structures.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing, ensure `flatten_include` continues to yield the composed values in a way compatible with PyYAML’s merge handling and keep existing merge-related tests passing.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering the key-order enforcement logic or error message structure.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests and downstream consumers may rely on exact substrings like 'keys out of order: expected ... got ... at ...'.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain the format of `ConstructorError.problem` and how context is computed from `node.start_mark` (line/column 1-based).

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying dump options or representer registrations in a way that changes emitted YAML formatting.
pitfalls.consequence: Exact string comparisons in tests for safe_dump output will fail (document start marker, quoting, ordering).
pitfalls.prevention: Keep `safe_dump`’s explicit kwargs (especially `explicit_start=True`, indent, flow style) and representer behavior stable.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing how include filenames are resolved from `mark.name`.
pitfalls.consequence: Includes may start resolving incorrectly when executed from different working directories or when parsing streams without expected mark metadata.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the current approach: `directory = dirname(mark.name)` then `join(directory, scalar)`.

key_sections: ContextLoader: !include constructors and mapping context wrapping
Flattener/merge integration: add_flattener, flatten_mapping, flatten_yaml_seq/map, flatten_include
SortEnforcingLoader + subclasses: order enforcement during construct_mapping
safe_dump + SortingDumper: key sorting during serialization and fixed formatting options
